name: Hypertension - Antihypertensive Medication Query (Multi-Class)
description: Tests ability to find hypertension patients via antihypertensive medication prescriptions. The PheKB resistant-hypertension algorithm requires 3-4 simultaneous antihypertensive classes. Modern HTN treatment spans ACE inhibitors, ARBs, calcium channel blockers, beta blockers, and diuretics — often used together. Synthea distributes patients across these 5 classes. An LLM querying only one class (e.g., lisinopril) will miss the substantial fraction on alternatives like losartan or amlodipine.
prompt: Find patients prescribed antihypertensive medications, including ACE inhibitors like lisinopril, ARBs like losartan, calcium channel blockers like amlodipine, beta blockers like carvedilol, or loop diuretics like furosemide.

prompts.naive: Find patients on blood pressure medications.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed any antihypertensive drug class — ACE inhibitors (e.g., lisinopril), angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs, e.g., losartan), calcium channel blockers (e.g., amlodipine), beta blockers (e.g., carvedilol), or diuretics (e.g., furosemide).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any antihypertensive class. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 29046 (lisinopril, ACE-I), 52175 (losartan, ARB), 17767 (amlodipine, CCB), 20352 (carvedilol, BB), 4603 (furosemide, loop diuretic). Combine in a single comma-separated code search.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with antihypertensive medication orders (Paths A and C). Captures both diagnosed HTN patients and Path C tricky patients on these drugs for non-HTN indications (carvedilol for HF, furosemide for edema, lisinopril for diabetic nephropathy, etc.).
